name: Circular route of the Montanuy Villages (PR-HU 101)
name_original: Ruta circular de los Pueblos de Montanuy (PR-HU 101)
region_intro: Hiking around Montanuy offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees. The region is characterized by rugged Pyrenean peaks, including views of the Maladeta massif, and features picturesque valleys like Baliera and Salenques. Rivers such as the Mijares have sculpted dramatic gorges, while high-mountain lakes, known as ibones, are found in areas like the Arnau valley and around the Llauset reservoir. Dense forests, including beech forests, contribute to the varied terrain. Best hiking trails around Montanuy The most popular hiking route is Salenques Beech Forest and Pi Waterfall Trail, a 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 46 minutes to complete. This moderate path leads through a scenic beech forest to the Cascadas del Pi. Another top favourite among local hikers is Llauset Lakes Loop, a difficult 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This route explores the high-mountain lakes (ibones) surrounding the Llauset reservoir. Local hikers also love the Refugio de Cap de Llauset from Embalse de Llauset, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through alpine terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Montanuy is defined by Pyrenean peaks, lush valleys, and high-mountain lakes. The network offers options for easy walks through forests to challenging ascents of significant summits. The routes in Montanuy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Montanuy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Montanuy?
A: Montanuy offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy walks through valleys to challenging ascents of Pyrenean peaks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Montanuy?
A: Hiking in Montanuy provides access to the stunning and varied landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees. You'll encounter rugged peaks, including views of the Maladeta massif, picturesque valleys like Baliera and Salenques, and dramatic gorges sculpted by rivers such as the Mijares. High-mountain lakes, known as ibones, are a prominent feature, particularly around the Llauset reservoir and in the Arnau valley. Dense forests, including beautiful beech forests, also contribute to the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Montanuy?
A: Yes, Montanuy offers several easier trails perfect for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1387360634/salenques-beech-forest-and-pi-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salenques Beech Forest and Pi Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 3.1-mile (4.9 km) path that leads through a scenic beech forest to the beautiful Cascadas del Pi, making it an enjoyable outing for all ages.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in Montanuy?
A: Yes, the region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1387360634/salenques-beech-forest-and-pi-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salenques Beech Forest and Pi Waterfall Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads to the Cascadas del Pi. Additionally, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/871651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ardonés Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6259947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remáscaro Gorge Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; in the surrounding natural monuments.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Montanuy?
A: Yes, Montanuy features several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943514271/llauset-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llauset Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 4.9-mile (7.9 km) path that explores the high-mountain lakes (ibones) surrounding the Llauset reservoir. Another challenging circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947531050/pico-vallhibierna-circular-from-refugio-de-cap-de-llauset" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Vallhibierna – circular from Refugio de Cap de Llauset&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Montanuy area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Montanuy offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1261770773/besiberri-sud-summit-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besiberri Sud Summit Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.6-mile (13.8 km) route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943514271/llauset-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llauset Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores high-mountain lakes. The ascent to Vallibierna peak is also a popular, difficult mountain hike.

Q: What points of interest or landmarks can I explore along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, some trails incorporate historical elements, such as the 'EL CASTILLO' trail which leads to the ruins of the 13th-century Aljama Castle, offering spectacular views. Natural points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/577388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forau de Aiguallut&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/347711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Aneto Summit&lt;/a&gt; (the highest in the Pyrenees), and various high-mountain lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3498342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cavallers Lake&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4853097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Gorgutes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Montanuy?
A: While specific public transport options directly to all trailheads in Montanuy can be limited due to its mountainous and rural nature, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider carpooling for access. Many trails are best reached by car, especially those in more remote high-mountain areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Montanuy?
A: The best time to hike in Montanuy generally depends on the desired experience. Late spring to early autumn (May to October) offers the most favorable conditions, with warmer weather and less snow, making high-mountain routes accessible. Summer provides lush landscapes and full access to all trails, while spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and beautiful seasonal colors. Winter hiking is possible but requires specialized gear and experience due to snow and ice.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Montanuy?
A: The trails in Montanuy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning Pyrenean peaks, the tranquility of the valleys, and the beauty of the high-mountain lakes. The diverse terrain, from dense beech forests to rugged alpine environments, is also a common highlight.

Q: Are there any multi-day trekking opportunities in the Montanuy region?
A: Yes, Montanuy is part of larger trekking routes, offering opportunities for multi-day adventures. The region is traversed by sections of the GR18, which connects various towns and natural sites. Additionally, stages of the 'El Gran Bucle' route, which extends through the Val d'Aran and Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, also pass through this area, providing options for extended hikes.

Q: Can I find places for wild swimming after a hike in Montanuy?
A: The landscape around Montanuy is crisscrossed by rivers and features numerous high-mountain lakes (ibones). While specific designated wild swimming spots are not always marked, the pristine rivers and natural pools along their courses, as well as some of the clearer ibones, can offer refreshing dips after a hike, especially during warmer months. Always exercise caution and respect local regulations regarding swimming in natural waters.
tips: This small town, with 7 registered inhabitants, belongs to the municipality of Montanuy. It has the remains of a Romanesque hermitage from the 12th century dedicated to San Pedro Mártir.
Also known as "Camino de la Serra", it was the old communication route between the border valley of Barrabés with that of Castanesa, through the Coll de Benifons.

This Ribagorzana town has about 50 inhabitants. Its main square is called "chicken" among its inhabitants. As an administrative peculiarity, its town hall is not located in the urban nucleus but on the N-260 highway, since it serves 17 population centers.
